steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says rising prices had a “counterintuitive effect of ______ demand.” We need a word meaning the rising prices produced or drew out new demand from buyers who had not previously been interested — the opposite of what normally happens.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'capitalizing': “capitalizing demand” is not a standard phrase and does not describe generating demand. ✗
  • Choice B — 'exploiting': to exploit demand means to take advantage of existing demand, not to create new demand. ✗
  • Choice C — 'eliciting': to elicit means to draw out or bring forth something that was not previously present — exactly what rising prices did when they drew new buyers into the market. ✓
  • Choice D — 'satisfying': to satisfy demand means to fulfill demand that already exists, not to generate new demand. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
